task_description:
Screen these abstracts for a systematic review on ML methods for predicting treatment response or disease progression in oncology. Produce a ranked top-5 shortlist of the most relevant and methodologically rigorous papers.

INCLUDE only papers that present or evaluate an ML model predicting treatment response (pathological complete response, tumour regression, response rate) or disease progression (recurrence, progression-free survival, time-to-progression) in cancer patients. Papers about cancer screening, diagnosis, drug discovery, radiotherapy planning, or non-cancer conditions should be EXCLUDE.

For each INCLUDE paper: assign rank 1 (best) to 5 (fifth-best) and write one justification sentence (max 200 chars).

Quality rubric: 1=no method; 2=vague; 3=clear metrics and dataset; 4=multiple baselines, external validation, ablation.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Lung Nodule Malignancy Classification in CT Screening
papers.abstract: We train a 3D CNN on NLST screening CT scans for lung nodule malignancy classification. AUC 0.94 is achieved, outperforming radiologist panel reads. This is cancer detection and risk classification for screening, not treatment response prediction in diagnosed patients.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, lung nodule, CT screening, malignancy classification
papers.methodology_hint: 3D CNN, NLST, AUC 0.94, screening diagnosis — not treatment response
papers.claimed_contribution: 3D CNN for lung nodule malignancy classification AUC 0.94, screening not treatment response.

papers.title: Multi-Omics Transformer for Predicting pCR to Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy in Breast Cancer
papers.abstract: Pathological complete response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy is the primary endpoint for early breast cancer treatment. We train a transformer integrating pre-treatment mRNA expression, copy number, and clinical stage from 1,800 patients to predict pCR. AUC 0.84 is achieved, outperforming single-omics models and an elasticnet baseline. An external TCGA-BRCA validation cohort confirms generalisability. Ablation of omics modalities quantifies each contribution.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, multi-omics, pCR prediction, neoadjuvant chemotherapy, breast cancer
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, 1800 patients, AUC 0.84, single-omics and elasticnet baselines, external validation, ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: Multi-omics transformer for breast cancer pCR prediction AUC 0.84, external validation, ablation.

papers.title: GAN for Synthetic CT Generation for Radiation Treatment Planning in Head-and-Neck Cancer
papers.abstract: MR-guided radiation therapy requires synthetic CT for dose calculation. We train a CycleGAN for MR-to-CT synthesis in head-and-neck cancer patients. Mean absolute error 71 HU is achieved. This is a radiotherapy planning tool; patient treatment response is not predicted.
papers.topic_hint: GAN, synthetic CT, MRI, radiotherapy planning, head-and-neck
papers.methodology_hint: CycleGAN, MR-to-CT synthesis, MAE 71 HU, radiation planning — not treatment response
papers.claimed_contribution: CycleGAN for MR-to-CT synthesis MAE 71 HU, radiation planning, not treatment response.

papers.title: Graph Neural Network for Predicting Immunotherapy Response in NSCLC from Spatial Proteomics
papers.abstract: Spatial proteomic features of the tumour microenvironment capture immune-tumour interactions not visible in bulk measurements. We construct spatial graphs from multiplexed IF images of 380 NSCLC biopsies and train a GNN to predict durable response to anti-PD-1 therapy. AUC 0.81 is achieved, outperforming PD-L1 IHC, TMB, and a spatial feature SVM baseline. Recursive feature elimination identifies CD8+ T-cell proximity to tumour cells as the strongest predictor.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, spatial proteomics, immunotherapy response, NSCLC, TME
papers.methodology_hint: GNN, 380 NSCLC biopsies, AUC 0.81, PD-L1/TMB/SVM baselines, feature importance
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN for NSCLC immunotherapy response prediction AUC 0.81, 3 baselines, TME feature importance.

papers.title: Convolutional Network for Automated Melanoma Diagnosis from Dermoscopy Images
papers.abstract: We train a CNN on 40,000 dermoscopy images for binary melanoma vs benign classification. AUC 0.97 is achieved, matching dermatologist consensus. This is a screening and diagnostic classification task; treatment response prediction is not addressed.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, melanoma diagnosis, dermoscopy, AUC, dermatologist comparison
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, 40k dermoscopy, AUC 0.97, diagnosis not treatment response
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN melanoma diagnosis from dermoscopy AUC 0.97, diagnostic not treatment prediction.

papers.title: Radiomic Machine Learning for Predicting Glioblastoma Progression-Free Survival from MRI
papers.abstract: We extract 120 radiomic features from pre-treatment MRI of 520 glioblastoma patients and train an SVM to predict progression-free survival. C-index 0.64 is achieved. Comparison against MGMT methylation status and clinical stage is provided. An external validation cohort from TCGA-GBM is included.
papers.topic_hint: radiomics, SVM, glioblastoma, progression-free survival, MRI
papers.methodology_hint: SVM, 520 GBM patients, C-index 0.64, MGMT and clinical baselines, TCGA external validation
papers.claimed_contribution: SVM radiomics for GBM progression-free survival C-index 0.64, MGMT comparison, external validation.

papers.title: Drug-Target Interaction Prediction Using Protein Language Models for Oncology Lead Optimisation
papers.abstract: We combine ESM-2 protein embeddings with molecular fingerprints to predict binding affinity for 240 oncology targets from ChEMBL. Pearson r=0.79 is achieved. This is a drug discovery application; patient treatment outcomes are not modelled.
papers.topic_hint: protein LM, drug-target affinity, oncology, drug discovery, ChEMBL
papers.methodology_hint: ESM-2, ChEMBL, Pearson 0.79, drug discovery — not patient treatment response
papers.claimed_contribution: ESM-2 for oncology drug-target affinity Pearson 0.79, drug discovery not treatment response.

papers.title: LSTM for Predicting Time-to-Progression in HER2-Positive Breast Cancer on Trastuzumab
papers.abstract: We train an LSTM on longitudinal tumour marker trajectories (CA 15-3, HER2-ECD) from 640 HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer patients on trastuzumab-based therapy to predict time-to-progression. C-index 0.72 is achieved, improving over a Cox regression on last observation by 0.09. Kaplan-Meier curves confirm risk group separation. No external validation is provided.
papers.topic_hint: LSTM, time-to-progression, HER2 breast cancer, trastuzumab, tumour markers
papers.methodology_hint: LSTM, 640 patients, C-index 0.72, Cox regression baseline, KM curves — no external validation
papers.claimed_contribution: LSTM for TTP prediction in HER2+ breast cancer on trastuzumab C-index 0.72, Cox comparison.

papers.title: Neural Network for Classifying Lung Cancer Histological Subtype from Cytology Slides
papers.abstract: Distinguishing adenocarcinoma from squamous cell carcinoma in cytology guides first-line therapy selection. We train a CNN on 8,200 cytology images to classify lung cancer subtype. Accuracy 0.91 is achieved. This classifies tumour subtype for initial diagnosis; it does not predict treatment response.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, lung cytology, histological subtype, adenocarcinoma, squamous
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, 8200 cytology images, accuracy 0.91, histological diagnosis not treatment response
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for lung cancer cytology subtype classification accuracy 0.91, diagnostic not predictive.

papers.title: Transformer for Predicting Recurrence in Stage II/III Colorectal Cancer After Surgery from Multi-Omics
papers.abstract: Post-surgical recurrence surveillance in colorectal cancer is imprecise with current biomarkers. We train a transformer on pre-surgical multi-omics profiles (mRNA, methylation, somatic mutations) from 1,100 stage II/III CRC patients to predict 3-year recurrence. AUC 0.81 is achieved, outperforming CEA, MSI status, and a multi-variable Cox model. Two independent external validation cohorts confirm results. Feature attribution analysis identifies KRAS/BRAF pathway activity as primary predictor.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, multi-omics, CRC recurrence, post-surgical prediction, stage II/III
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, 1100 CRC patients, AUC 0.81, CEA/MSI/Cox baselines, 2 external validations, KRAS attribution
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for CRC recurrence prediction AUC 0.81, 3 baselines, 2 external validations, feature attribution.

papers.title: Random Forest for Acute Myeloid Leukaemia Complete Remission Prediction from EHR
papers.abstract: Predicting which AML patients will achieve complete remission after induction chemotherapy supports treatment stratification. We train a random forest on 1,800 AML patient records using age, cytogenetics, and pre-treatment lab values to predict CR. AUC 0.76 is achieved. Only logistic regression is compared; no external validation is included.
papers.topic_hint: random forest, AML, complete remission, induction chemotherapy, treatment response
papers.methodology_hint: random forest, 1800 AML patients, AUC 0.76, only LR comparison, no external validation
papers.claimed_contribution: Random forest for AML CR prediction AUC 0.76, treatment response, limited validation.

papers.title: Bayesian Optimisation for Personalised Immunotherapy Combination Dosing
papers.abstract: Combining checkpoint inhibitors with different doses is a complex optimisation problem. We apply Bayesian optimisation to personalise dual immunotherapy dosing schedules in a synthetic patient simulation based on pharmacokinetic models. This is a treatment optimisation simulation study; patient clinical outcomes are not predicted from real data.
papers.topic_hint: Bayesian optimisation, immunotherapy dosing, personalised treatment, pharmacokinetics
papers.methodology_hint: Bayesian optimisation, synthetic simulation, dosing optimisation — no clinical outcome prediction from real data
papers.claimed_contribution: Bayesian optimisation for immunotherapy dosing personalisation, synthetic simulation, no clinical prediction.

papers.title: Gradient Boosting for Early Prediction of Chemotherapy-Induced Neuropathy
papers.abstract: Peripheral neuropathy is a common dose-limiting toxicity of taxane and platinum chemotherapy. We train gradient boosting on pre-treatment clinical and pharmacogenomic features from 2,400 patients across four cancer types to predict grade 3+ chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy. AUC 0.77 is achieved. This predicts treatment toxicity, not treatment response or tumour progression.
papers.topic_hint: gradient boosting, chemotherapy toxicity, neuropathy, pharmacogenomics
papers.methodology_hint: gradient boosting, 2400 patients, AUC 0.77, toxicity prediction — not tumour response
papers.claimed_contribution: Gradient boosting for chemotherapy neuropathy toxicity prediction AUC 0.77, toxicity not response.

papers.title: DeepHit for Overall Survival Prediction in Pancreatic Cancer from CT Radiomics and Pathology
papers.abstract: Pancreatic cancer has poor survival and better prognostic stratification is needed. We train a DeepHit neural survival model combining CT radiomic features and pathological staging from 680 pancreatic cancer patients to predict overall survival. C-index 0.70 is achieved, outperforming Cox with the same features and a clinical staging model. External validation on a separate registry confirms C-index 0.68.
papers.topic_hint: DeepHit, pancreatic cancer, overall survival, CT radiomics, prognosis
papers.methodology_hint: DeepHit, 680 patients, C-index 0.70, Cox and clinical staging baselines, external validation C-index 0.68
papers.claimed_contribution: DeepHit for pancreatic cancer OS prediction C-index 0.70, 2 baselines, external validation.

papers.title: Deep Learning Identifies Tumour-Infiltrating Lymphocyte Density for Predicting Chemotherapy Benefit
papers.abstract: TIL density is an immunological biomarker correlated with chemotherapy benefit in triple-negative breast cancer. We train a CNN on H&E slides from 1,400 TNBC patients to automatically quantify TIL density and use it to stratify patients by predicted chemotherapy benefit. The model predicts benefit from chemotherapy using a pathology-derived biomarker, not a direct treatment response predictor.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, TIL density, triple-negative breast cancer, H&E, chemotherapy benefit
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, TIL quantification, 1400 TNBC, chemotherapy benefit stratification — biomarker not direct response
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for TIL quantification predicting chemotherapy benefit in TNBC, biomarker-based stratification.

papers.title: XGBoost for Predicting Immunotherapy Objective Response Rate in Melanoma from Baseline Features
papers.abstract: Predicting objective response to anti-PD-1 monotherapy before treatment in advanced melanoma is clinically valuable. We train XGBoost on pre-treatment LDH, tumour burden, ECOG status, and PD-L1 expression from 820 patients across three clinical centres. AUC 0.74 for objective response (PR+CR) prediction is achieved, outperforming PD-L1 threshold and logistic regression. Cross-centre validation confirms generalisability.
papers.topic_hint: XGBoost, melanoma, immunotherapy response, objective response rate, anti-PD-1
papers.methodology_hint: XGBoost, 820 melanoma patients, AUC 0.74, PD-L1 and LR baselines, cross-centre validation
papers.claimed_contribution: XGBoost for melanoma immunotherapy objective response prediction AUC 0.74, cross-centre validation.

papers.title: Attention CNN for Classifying Renal Cell Carcinoma Subtype from CT Without Biopsy
papers.abstract: Non-invasive subtype classification guides treatment planning for RCC. We train an attention CNN on 1,200 renal CT volumes for papillary vs clear cell vs chromophobe classification. AUC 0.89 is achieved. This classifies tumour subtype for diagnosis; it does not predict treatment response or disease progression.
papers.topic_hint: attention CNN, renal cell carcinoma, CT, subtype classification
papers.methodology_hint: attention CNN, 1200 CT volumes, AUC 0.89, subtype diagnosis not treatment response
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention CNN for RCC subtype classification AUC 0.89, diagnostic not treatment response.

papers.title: Survival Transformer for Predicting Progression-Free Survival in Multiple Myeloma from Immunophenotyping
papers.abstract: Multiple myeloma is characterised by clonal plasma cell proliferation with variable progression rates. We train a survival transformer on flow cytometry immunophenotyping data from 920 myeloma patients at diagnosis to predict PFS. C-index 0.74 is achieved, outperforming ISS staging alone and a Cox regression on the same features. An ablation quantifies the contribution of surface marker profiles versus clonality metrics.
papers.topic_hint: survival transformer, multiple myeloma, PFS, flow cytometry, immunophenotyping
papers.methodology_hint: survival transformer, 920 myeloma patients, C-index 0.74, ISS and Cox baselines, ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: Survival transformer for myeloma PFS prediction C-index 0.74, ISS and Cox baselines, ablation.

papers.title: Survey of ML for Treatment Response Prediction in Oncology: 90 Papers
papers.abstract: We survey 90 papers applying ML to treatment response prediction across cancer types, analysing data modality, algorithm choice, and validation strategy. Key gaps identified include limited external validation and underrepresentation of rare cancers. No new model is developed.
papers.topic_hint: survey, treatment response, oncology ML, 90 papers
papers.methodology_hint: survey, 90 papers, no new model
papers.claimed_contribution: Survey of 90 treatment response ML papers, no new model.

papers.title: LSTM for Predicting Objective Response in HCC Patients on Sorafenib from Longitudinal AFP Trajectories
papers.abstract: Alpha-fetoprotein kinetics during sorafenib therapy may predict objective response in hepatocellular carcinoma. We train an LSTM on weekly AFP measurements from 480 HCC patients on sorafenib to predict objective response at 12 weeks. AUC 0.79 is achieved, improving over AFP-response threshold by 0.14. External validation on an Asian registry cohort is provided. No ablation or multi-baseline comparison is reported.
papers.topic_hint: LSTM, HCC, sorafenib response, AFP trajectory, treatment prediction
papers.methodology_hint: LSTM, 480 HCC patients, AUC 0.79, AFP threshold comparison, external validation — no ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: LSTM for HCC sorafenib response prediction from AFP kinetics AUC 0.79, external validation.
